39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
64	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
65	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
66	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
67	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
68	
69	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
70	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
71	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
72			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
73	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
74	
75	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
76	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
77	
78	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
79	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
80	
81	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
82	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
83			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
84			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
85		}
86		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
87		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
88		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
89		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
90		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
91		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
92		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
93		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
94		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
96		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
97		return cmd
98	}

100	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
101		beadID := args[0]
102		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
103			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
104		}
105		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
106		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
107		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
108		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
109		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
110		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
111		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
112		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
113		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
114		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
115		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
116	
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
120			Harness:       harness,
121			Model:         model,
122			Provider:      provider,
123			ModelRef:      modelRef,
124			Effort:        effort,
125			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
126		})
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221			} else if err == nil {
222				err = landErr
223			}
224		}
225	
226		if err != nil {
227			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
228			return err
229		}
230	
231		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
232	}

234	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
235		if res == nil {
236			return nil
237		}
238		if asJSON {
239			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
240			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
241			return enc.Encode(res)
242		}
243	
244		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
245		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
246		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
247			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
248		}
249		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
250		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
251		if res.Detail != "" {
252			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
253		}
254		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
255			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
256		}
257		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
258			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
259		}
260		return nil
261	}

1607	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1608	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1609	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1610	
1611	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1612	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1613	
1614	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1615	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1616	
1617	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1618	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1619	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1620	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1621	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1622	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1623	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1624	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1625	
1626	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1627	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1628	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1629	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1630	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1631	
1632	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1633	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1634	process.
1635	
1636	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1637	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1638	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1639	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory

1641	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1642	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1643	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1644	`,
